div.head: The Ambassador in France (Straus) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 708. Department’s telegram No. 393, September 27, 7 p.m.Not printed; it reported information contained in first paragraph of tele gram No. 311, September 27, 4 p.m., to the Minister in China, p. 538. During the course of a conversation between RogersAlan Stewart Rogers, Third Secretary of Embassy in France. and Decloux of the American section of the Foreign Office on whom Rogers called yesterday afternoon on routine business Decloux of his own accord brought up the question of Italy raising its Legation in Peiping to the grade of Embassy. Decloux said that he thought his Government was very embarrassed and that the British were particularly so. He said that he understood that the British had lodged a formal protest through their Ambassador at Rome. He said further that he believed this to be an Italian gesture to flatter Chinese pride and thus assure for themselves a favored commercial position in China. Rogers made no comment.
